stages.purpose: Search both name-and-location pairs in the same assistant action; all inputs are known and neither search depends on the other.
Only after stage 1 returns one match for a branch, use that returned ID in a later assistant action; when both branches resolve, the two gets are independent and belong together.

state_assertions: No record, notification log, or mutation log changes.
sup-905 and sup-805 remain untouched distractors.
Each get ID equals the sole match returned by its own search; no ID is inferred from the prompt or fixture.
response_constraints: Base the comparison only on successful get responses, not on abbreviated search matches.
For a branch with zero or multiple search matches, omit its get; still complete any independent branch with exactly one match and identify the unresolved branch.
Do not expose distractor records or propose or perform changes.
List the earlier-dated record first, the later-dated record second, then add one sentence comparing their statuses.

prompt: The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. The brief needs evidence from full records rather than search snippets. Find the case named “Damaged Cold-Chain Parcel” at “Alder Foods” and the case named “Duplicate Annual Charge” at “Beacon Arts”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. List the earlier-dated record first, the later-dated record second, then add one sentence comparing their statuses.
